## Context

  "text": "Apache Kafka implements log compaction through a sophisticated multi-threaded architecture. The core components are located in the storage module at org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/.\n\n## Cleaner Thread Implementation\nThe LogCleaner.java file contains the main cleaner thread management. It implements BrokerReconfigurable and manages a pool of CleanerThread instances that extend ShutdownableThread. Each CleanerThread runs a doWork() method that continuously selects and cleans logs.\n\n## Segment Selection Policy\nThe segment selection follows a \"dirtiness\" ratio strategy implemented in LogCleanerManager.java. The grabFilthiestCompactedLog() method selects the log partition with the highest ratio of dirty (uncleanable) bytes to total bytes. LogToClean.java encapsulates a log's cleanability state with calculations for cleanableRatio, which is used in the Comparable implementation to sort logs by dirtiness. The segment grouping strategy is implemented in Cleaner.java's groupSegmentsBySize() method, which groups consecutive segments to be cleaned together based on size constraints.\n\n## Key-Based Deduplication Logic\nThe deduplication uses an OffsetMap interface (OffsetMap.java) that provides a key=>offset mapping. The primary implementation is SkimpyOffsetMap.java, which uses a cryptographically secure hash (MD5) as a proxy for keys to save memory. During cleaning:\n\n1. buildOffsetMap() in Cleaner.java builds a map of all keys from the dirty section of the log\n2. shouldRetainRecord() checks if a record should be retained by querying the OffsetMap\n3. This effectively removes all but the latest record for each key\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\nCleanedTransactionMetadata.java handles special logic for transactional records, ensuring transaction markers and aborted transaction records are properly managed.
